arguments.prompt:
Review all MSBuild project files in this .NET solution for anti-patterns, duplication, modernization opportunities, and best practices violations. The solution is at /tmp/sv-74374718b263492d966aa0ab3059e706/ContosoApp.sln with source in src/ and tests in test/.

Specifically look for:
1. Duplicated properties across project files (TargetFramework, LangVersion, Nullable, ImplicitUsings, common package versions, etc.)
2. Opportunities to introduce Directory.Build.props / Directory.Build.targets to centralize settings
3. Opportunities to use NuGet Central Package Management (Directory.Packages.props)
4. Anti-patterns like hardcoded paths, restating SDK defaults, scattered package versions
5. Any modernization opportunities (legacy patterns that could be simplified)
6. Build organization improvements for maintainability

Scan all .csproj, .props, .targets, .sln files and produce actionable improvement suggestions with concrete before/after examples.

I have all the information needed from reading the project files. Here's my comprehensive review:

---

## MSBuild Review: ContosoApp Solution

### Key Findings

**Every project repeats these 6 properties identically:**
- `TargetFramework`: net8.0
- `Nullable`: enable
- `ImplicitUsings`: enable
- `TreatWarningsAsErrors`: true
- `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild`: true
- `Company`/`Authors`: Contoso / Contoso Engineering

**Package version drift detected:**
- `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions`: **8.0.0** (Api, Core) vs **8.0.1** (Services)
- Test packages diverge between test projects (`Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` 17.9.0 vs 17.10.0, `xunit` 2.7.1 vs 2.8.1)

**Duplicated custom target** — `PrintBuildInfo` is copy-pasted in all 3 src projects.

**Duplicated `DocumentationFile`** setting in all 3 src projects.

---

### Recommended Changes

#### 1. Create `Directory.Build.props` (repo root)

Centralizes all shared properties in one place:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- All test projects are non-packable -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

#### 2. Create `Directory.Build.targets` (repo root)

Moves the shared custom target and documentation settings:

```xml
<Project>
  <!-- Generate XML docs for src projects only -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build"
          Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>
</Project>
```

#### 3. Create `Directory.Packages.props` (Central Package Management)

Pins all versions in one file and eliminates version drift:

```xml
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <!-- Test -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

#### 4. Simplified Project Files (after changes)

**`src/ContosoApp.Core/ContosoApp.Core.csproj`** — from 26 lines to:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

**`src/ContosoApp.Services/ContosoApp.Services.csproj`** — from 30 lines to:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="Newtonsoft.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

**`test/ContosoApp.Core.Tests/ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj`** — from 25 lines to:
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>
```

---

### Summary of Issues by Severity

| Issue | Impact |
|-------|--------|
| **Version drift** (`Logging.Abstractions` 8.0.0 vs 8.0.1) | 🔴 Potential runtime conflicts |
| **Duplicated properties** (6 props × 5 projects = 30 redundant lines) | 🟡 Maintenance burden |
| **Duplicated `PrintBuildInfo` target** (3 copies) | 🟡 Divergence risk |
| **No central package management** | 🟡 Version drift will worsen |
| **Test package version mismatch** (xunit 2.7.1 vs 2.8.1) | 🟠 Inconsistent test behavior |

### Net Result

- **Before**: ~140 lines of project XML across 5 files, with significant duplication
- **After**: ~50 lines across 5 project files + 3 shared infrastructure files
- All versions pinned centrally; adding a new project requires only its unique dependencies

Would you like me to implement these changes?

**Project Structure:**
- 5 projects: 3 src (Core, Services, Api) + 2 test (Core.Tests, Api.Tests)
- All SDK-style projects ✅ (no legacy)
- No Directory.Build.props, Directory.Build.targets, or Directory.Packages.props

**Duplicated Properties (across ALL 5 projects):**
- `TargetFramework` = `net8.0` — in all 5
- `Nullable` = `enable` — in all 5
- `ImplicitUsings` = `enable` — in all 5


- `TreatWarningsAsErrors` = `true` — in all 5
- `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` = `true` — in all 5
- `Company` = `Contoso` — in all 5
- `Authors` = `Contoso Engineering` — in all 5

The src projects all define XML documentation output paths and share an identical build info target, while the test projects both have packaging disabled.

Now I'm noticing significant version inconsistencies across the test dependencies. Several packages are pinned to different versions — `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` ranges from 8.0.0 to 8.0.1, the test SDK varies between 17.10.0 and 17.9.0, and xunit is split between 2.8.1 and 2.7.1 across the test projects. The runner and System.Text.Json also show drift.

Looking at the structural problems, there's massive duplication without a centralized build configuration — no Directory.Build.props or Central Package Management. The `PrintBuildInfo` target and `DocumentationFile` settings are repeated across multiple projects when they should be consolidated. Several projects have multiple PropertyGroups that could be merged, and the test runner package is missing the `PrivateAssets="all"` metadata to prevent it from leaking into consuming projects.

The key finding is a version mismatch on `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` (8.0.0 vs 8.0.1) that could cause runtime issues, plus scattered package versions across test projects and duplicated metadata like `Company` and `Authors` that belong in a shared build file.

To fix this, I should introduce a Directory.Build.props at the repo root for centralized package management, add Directory.Build.targets for shared build logic like the `PrintBuildInfo` target, and use Directory.Packages.props to manage all package versions in one place. The good news is all projects are already SDK-style with consistent compiler settings and proper test project configuration, so the foundation is solid.

data.content:
I have all the data needed for a complete review. Here is the full analysis.

---

# MSBuild Project Review — ContosoApp Solution

## Solution Topology

| Project | SDK | Type |
|---|---|---|
| `src/ContosoApp.Core` | `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` | Class library |
| `src/ContosoApp.Services` | `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` | Class library (→ Core) |
| `src/ContosoApp.Api` | `Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web` | Web API (→ Core, Services) |
| `test/ContosoApp.Core.Tests` | `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` | Test (→ Core) |
| `test/ContosoApp.Api.Tests` | `Microsoft.NET.Sdk` | Test (→ Api) |

No `Directory.Build.props`, `Directory.Build.targets`, or `Directory.Packages.props` exist. No `global.json` or `.editorconfig`.

---

## 🔴 Errors (1)

### E1: Package Version Drift on `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions`

The same package is referenced at **two different versions** across projects:

| Project | Version |
|---|---|
| `ContosoApp.Core` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Api` | **8.0.0** |
| `ContosoApp.Services` | **8.0.1** |

Since `Api` references both `Core` (8.0.0) and `Services` (8.0.1), NuGet will resolve to 8.0.1 at the `Api` level, but this silent version unification is fragile. If a breaking behavioral change ships in the patch, `Core` was tested against 8.0.0 but runs with 8.0.1. This class of bug is exactly what Central Package Management prevents.

---

## 🟡 Warnings (4)

### W1: Seven Properties Copy-Pasted Across All 5 Projects

Every single `.csproj` contains this identical block:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

That's **35 lines of duplication** (7 properties × 5 projects). When you need to upgrade to `net9.0`, you must edit 5 files and hope nobody misses one. This should be a single `Directory.Build.props`.

### W2: Test Package Versions Diverged Between Test Projects

| Package | Core.Tests | Api.Tests |
|---|---|---|
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | **17.10.0** | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit` | **2.8.1** | **2.7.1** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | **2.8.1** | **2.5.6** |
| `NSubstitute` | 5.1.0 | 5.1.0 |

This is a maintenance hazard — one test project was updated and the other wasn't. Tests may behave differently across projects for reasons unrelated to the code under test.

### W3: `PrintBuildInfo` Target Duplicated in 3 Source Projects

The exact same target appears in `Core.csproj`, `Services.csproj`, and `Api.csproj`:

```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

This belongs in a `Directory.Build.targets` file.

### W4: `DocumentationFile` Property Duplicated in 3 Source Projects

All three source projects contain:

```xml
<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
```

This should also be centralized. Alternatively, the modern SDK equivalent is simply `<GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>` (which auto-computes the path) — see suggestion S4 below.

---

## 🔵 Suggestions (6)

### S1: Introduce `Directory.Build.props` at Repository Root

**Consolidates W1, W4, and partially W2.** Every project gets these properties automatically.

**Proposed `Directory.Build.props`:**
```xml
<Project>

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Source projects generate XML docs -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Test projects are never packable -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

**After: `ContosoApp.Core.csproj` shrinks to:**
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
    <PackageReference Include="System.Text.Json" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

**After: `ContosoApp.Core.Tests.csproj` shrinks to:**
```xml
<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">

  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageReference Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit" />
    <PackageReference Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" />
    <PackageReference Include="NSubstitute" />
  </ItemGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <ProjectReference Include="..\..\src\ContosoApp.Core\ContosoApp.Core.csproj" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

(Versions removed from `PackageReference` — controlled centrally via CPM, see S3.)

### S2: Introduce `Directory.Build.targets` at Repository Root

**Consolidates W3.** Targets *must* go in `.targets`, not `.props`, because they execute after project evaluation.

**Proposed `Directory.Build.targets`:**
```xml
<Project>

  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>

</Project>
```

Then **remove** the `<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" ...>` block from all three source `.csproj` files.

### S3: Introduce `Directory.Packages.props` for Central Package Management

**Resolves E1 and W2** — all version drift is eliminated at the source.

**Proposed `Directory.Packages.props`:**
```xml
<Project>

  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- Shared -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />

    <!-- Testing -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
  </ItemGroup>

</Project>
```

Then **remove all `Version="..."` attributes** from every `<PackageReference>` in every `.csproj`. For example:

**Before** (in `ContosoApp.Services.csproj`):
```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
```

**After:**
```xml
<PackageReference Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" />
```

### S4: Replace `DocumentationFile` with `GenerateDocumentationFile`

The pattern used in all three source projects:
```xml
<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
```
is manually restating what the SDK does when you simply set:
```xml
<GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
```

The SDK default already puts the XML file at `$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml`. Using the modern property is cleaner and less error-prone (e.g., the manual path doesn't account for multi-targeting output paths). Already included in S1 above.

### S5: Consolidate Split `PropertyGroup` Elements

`Core.csproj`, `Services.csproj`, and `Api.csproj` each have **two `<PropertyGroup>` elements** with no condition differentiating them. The second one only contains `DocumentationFile`. These should be merged into one `<PropertyGroup>` (or eliminated entirely with S1). This is a readability/organization improvement.

**Before** (e.g., `ContosoApp.Core.csproj`):
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  <!-- ...7 properties... -->
</PropertyGroup>
<!-- ...ItemGroups... -->
<PropertyGroup>
  <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

**After** (if not using `Directory.Build.props`):
```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
  <!-- ...7 properties... -->
  <GenerateDocumentationFile>true</GenerateDocumentationFile>
</PropertyGroup>
```

### S6: Add a `global.json` to Pin the SDK Version

There is no `global.json`. Without it, builds use whatever SDK is installed, which can cause inconsistencies across developer machines and CI. For a `net8.0` solution:

```json
{
  "sdk": {
    "version": "8.0.100",
    "rollForward": "latestFeature"
  }
}
```

---

## 🟢 Positive Observations

| Practice | Status |
|---|---|
| All projects use SDK-style format | ✅ Modern |
| `TreatWarningsAsErrors` enabled everywhere | ✅ Excellent |
| `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` enabled everywhere | ✅ Excellent |
| `Nullable` enabled everywhere | ✅ Best practice |
| Test projects marked `IsPackable=false` | ✅ Correct |
| Clean ProjectReference graph (no circular deps) | ✅ Clean |
